new_classes.label_en: Rum and Other Spirits from Fermented Sugar-Cane Products in containers > 2 L (CN 2208 40 91)
new_classes.label_de: Rum und andere Branntweine aus vergorenen Zuckerrohrerzeugnissen in Behältnissen > 2 L (KN 2208 40 91)
new_classes.definition_en: Spirit obtained by distilling fermented sugar-cane products (such as rum and taffia), possessing typical organoleptic characteristics derived from those products, presented in containers of more than 2 litres.

coverage_explanation: The key distinguishing criteria from the legal text are: (1) the spirit is obtained by distilling fermented sugar-cane products (captured via eucn:SugarCaneFermentationProcess and eucn:RumAndSugarCaneSpirit parent class); (2) it must possess typical organoleptic/sensory characteristics of the product type (captured via eucn:OrganolepticCharacteristicsQuality); (3) it is presented in containers of more than 2 L (captured via eucn:LargeContainer, which contrasts with the sibling eucn:RumAndSugarCaneSpiritSmallContainer at CN 2208 40 51). The disjointness complement restriction with the small-container sibling discriminates this node from its sibling. The coverage is slightly below 1.0 because the legal text specifically mentions rum and taffia as examples with HS explanatory note references (third paragraph, point 3 of HS position 2208), and there is no finer-grained class or property in the TBox to capture the full range of named product types (taffia) or the specific HS explanatory note reference. The container-volume threshold (> 2 L vs ≤ 2 L) is the primary discriminator at this level of the CN hierarchy and is well covered.
